# checkpointsase_firewall_policy (Resource)

Manages the auto-created firewall policy of a `checkpointsase_network` or `checkpointsase_enhanced_network`. **This is an adopt-style resource**: the firewall policy is created server-side automatically when the network is provisioned, so terraform `create` here actually *adopts* the existing policy and applies your config; `destroy` simply releases the policy from terraform state without removing it server-side (the policy continues to exist for the lifetime of its parent network). Use this resource to manage the policy's `enabled` / `allowed` defaults and to declare `policy_rules`. **`network_id` is immutable** — changing it forces resource replacement.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "checkpointsase_regions" "all" {}

resource "checkpointsase_network" "example" {
  network {
    name   = "tfExampleNet"
    subnet = "10.0.0.0/16"
  }
  region {
    cpregion_id = data.checkpointsase_regions.all.regions[0].id
  }
}

# Adopt-style: a firewall policy is auto-created with the network.
# Importing it under terraform lets you manage its enabled / allowed flags.
resource "checkpointsase_firewall_policy" "example" {
  network_id = checkpointsase_network.example.id
  enabled    = true
  allowed    = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `allowed` (Boolean) Whether the default policy action is allow (true) or drop (false).
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the firewall policy is enabled.
- `network_id` (String) The ID of the network whose firewall policy to manage.

### Optional

- `policy_rules` (Block List) List of firewall policy rules. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--policy_rules))
- `trace` (Boolean) Whether the policy is traced.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.

<a id="nestedblock--policy_rules"></a>
### Nested Schema for `policy_rules`

Required:

- `allowed` (Boolean) Whether this rule allows (true) or denies (false) the traffic.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether this rule is enabled.
- `name` (String) The name of the policy rule.

Optional:

- `id` (String) The unique ID of the policy rule.
- `services` (List of String) List of service object IDs to match in this rule.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

```shell
# Import an existing firewall_policy resource by its ID.
terraform import checkpointsase_firewall_policy.example <id>
```
